news.summary: In Plantation, Fla., and Bentonville, Ark., a partnership between NationsBenefits® and Walmart has been established to revolutionize access to crucial benefits for health plan members through technological solutions. NationsBenefits® is a prominent healthcare fintech, supplemental benefits, and outcomes platform, while Walmart is recognized as the biggest retailer globally. The collaboration aims to enhance the way individuals access necessary benefits, utilizing NationsBenefits’ Basket Analyzer Service (BAS) and Walmart Everyday Health Signals™ program. This joint effort signifies a significant step towards providing improved services to plan members and is a testament to the commitment of both entities to innovate the healthcare industry.
news.headline: NationsBenefits and Walmart team up to enhance health plan engagement through tech integration, focusing on Basket Analyzer Service.

news.summary: A technology company based in San Francisco, DreamPark, has revealed that it has successfully raised $1.1 million in seed funding. This financial boost was spearheaded by Long Journey Ventures, with additional contributions from Founders Inc. DreamPark is renowned for being the creator of the world's largest downloadable mixed reality theme park. Their primary goal is to revamp mundane surroundings into thrilling adventures using cutting-edge mixed reality (XR) technology. By obtaining this investment, DreamPark aims to accelerate its efforts in making Earth an exciting playground once again. The company stands out as a leader in mixed reality innovation, continuously pushing boundaries in this field.
news.headline: DreamPark gets $1.1M seed funding to convert real spaces to mixed reality parks, led by Long Journey Ventures and Founders Inc.

news.summary: Minneapolis-based retail giant Best Buy Co., Inc. (NYSE: BBY) released its financial results for the first quarter ending on May 3, 2025 (“Q1 FY26”), in comparison to the same period in fiscal year 2024 (“Q1 FY25”). Total revenue in Q1 FY26 amounted to $8.767 billion for the Enterprise, slightly lower than the $8.847 billion reported in Q1 FY25. In terms of segments, the Domestic sector saw revenues of $8.127 billion (compared to $8.203 billion in Q1 FY25) while the International segment brought in $640 million (down from $644 million in Q1 FY25). The Enterprise comparable sales witnessed a 0.7% decrease in Q1 FY26, compared to a 6.1% decline in Q1 FY25. Similarly, Domestic comparable sales dipped by 0.7% in Q1 FY26, in contrast to a 6.3% drop noted in Q1 FY25.
news.headline: Best Buy's Q1 FY26 revenue: $8.767B, down from $8.847B in Q1 FY25 with comparable sales % change at -0.7% domestically.

news.summary: DCG, a worldwide investor, creator, and nurturer of decentralized ecosystems, has disclosed findings from a recent study in collaboration with The Harris Poll. The research indicates that 75% of consumers have faith in decentralized AI to bolster innovation and advancement over centralized AI. The survey reached 2,036 U.S. participants, including registered voters and AI users, showcasing a rising preference for decentralized artificial intelligence strategies.
news.headline: 75% of Americans prefer decentralized AI innovation over centralized AI, as revealed in DCG and The Harris Poll study.

news.summary: Fifth Third Bank has been named as having the best mobile banking app experience among regional banks in the US, according to J.D. Power's 2025 U.S. Banking Mobile App Satisfaction Study. This recognition includes being rated No. 1 for Visual Appeal by study respondents. Ben Hoffman, the Chief Strategy Officer and Head of Consumer Products at Fifth Third, expressed gratitude for the acknowledgment from customers, emphasizing the commitment of their digital teams to providing top-notch, seamless experiences. The bank's achievement is particularly notable given the competitive landscape of mobile banking apps.
news.headline: Fifth Third Bank ranked #1 in J.D. Power's 2025 U.S. Banking Mobile App Satisfaction StudySM, praised for visual appeal.

news.summary: In Montreal, as per a survey conducted in March 2025 by Arlington Research, 1,500 Canadian adults who had flown at least once in the past two years were polled, revealing shifting financial priorities. In the survey, it was uncovered that Canadians are facing dilemmas when it comes to prioritizing significant expenditures. The research by FlightHub delved into the attitudes of Canadians towards travel in comparison to other major life milestones like homeownership. This suggests a growing trend of economic uncertainty influencing decision-making processes, highlighting the challenges individuals are encountering in navigating financial choices.
news.headline: Canadians face tough financial decisions amid uncertain times. FlightHub survey polls 1,500 adults in Canada on travel vs. homeownership priorities.

news.summary: In a recent development, Align Capital Partners (ACP) has successfully finalized a growth investment in Strata Landscape Services, a prominent provider of commercial landscape services in the Western region of the United States. The company, led by seasoned professionals Jerry DeJournett as CEO, Ben Green as COO, and Justin Crandall as CFO, was established in 2024 through the acquisition of Verdant Landscaping based in Southern California. This strategic partnership signifies a significant move in the sector, with ACP providing vital support and resources for Strata's expansion.
news.headline: Align Capital Partners invests in Strata Landscape Services, a top commercial landscape provider in Western US.

news.summary: Bath & Body Works reported a 3% increase in net sales to $1.4 billion in the first quarter of 2025, with earnings per share rising by 29% to $0.49 exceeding high-end guidance. The company maintains its 2025 guidance of 1-3% growth in net sales and earnings per share between $3.25 to $3.60. The company's new CEO, Daniel Heaf, expressed confidence in the brand's strategic position and its ability to accelerate growth globally. Operating income for the quarter was $209 million and net income reached $105 million. The company anticipates flat to 2% growth in net sales for the second quarter of 2025, and earnings per diluted share between $0.33 and $0.38. These figures are inclusive of current tariff rates and do not consider the impact of the CEO transition on earnings per diluted share, which is estimated at approximately $0.05. The company expects to generate free cash flow of $750 million to $850 million in fiscal 2025. For further details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bath & Body Works reports 2025 Q1 results with net sales up 3% to $1.4B and EPS up 29% to $0.49, maintains fiscal year 2025 guidance. CEO transition announced. For more info, visit Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In recent data released, it was found that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.8%. The report also indicated that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in the last month, surpassing expectations set by experts. Additionally, the average hourly wage increased by 3.2% compared to the previous year. This positive trend in job growth and wage increase has been warmly welcomed by policymakers and economists alike.
news.headline: Discussion starts on the economic effects of 'Military Keynesianism'; analysis focuses on significant numbers.

news.summary: Raised by a cardiac surgeon, Michael Mahoney initially aspired to follow in his grandfather's footsteps and become a doctor. However, he eventually shifted his career trajectory and found success in the business world. Mahoney played a pivotal role in transforming Boston Scientific into a dominant force in the medical technology industry. This story highlights the importance of adaptability and seizing new opportunities for professional growth.
news.headline: Michael Mahoney transformed Boston Scientific into a medical tech giant, changing career path from doctor to CEO.

news.summary: April 2025 saw a significant achievement in the industry as a record-breaking 300 metric tons of liquid hydrogen were successfully produced. This milestone indicates a remarkable leap in production capabilities, showcasing the advancement and growth of the industry. The substantial output of liquid hydrogen reflects the increasing demand and utilization of this energy source, highlighting its growing importance in various sectors. This achievement demonstrates a promising future for the industry and signifies a notable step forward in meeting energy needs efficiently and sustainably.
news.headline: Plug Power's Georgia plant achieves record US production with 300 metric tons of liquid hydrogen in April 2025.

news.summary: Cullinan Therapeutics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company traded on Nasdaq under the symbol CGEM, is set to host a company event at the 2025 American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Annual Meeting in Chicago, IL. The management team will also take part in the Jefferies 2025 Global Healthcare Conference in New York, NY from June 3-5, 2025. These events serve as platforms for Cullinan to showcase its focus on developing targeted therapies that transcend different treatment modalities.
news.headline: Cullinan to host event at 2025 ASCO meeting and join Jefferies 2025 Global Healthcare Conference.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Tilray Brands, Inc. (NASDAQ: TLRY, TSX: TLRY) headquartered in New York and Leamington, Ontario, announced that their Chairman and CEO, Irwin D. Simon, along with CFO Carl Merton, will be taking part in a fireside chat and hosting individual meetings at the TD Cowen 9th Annual Future of the Consumer Conference in New York, NY on June 3, 2025. The fireside chat is set for 11:00 a.m. ET, with a webcast accessible on Tilray's Investor Relations website. Tilray is a global company operating in the lifestyle, consumer packaged goods, beverage, cannabis, and wellness sectors.
news.headline: Tilray Brands to present at TD Cowen's annual conference on June 3, 2025, with CEO and CFO participating in a fireside chat at 11 am ET.

news.summary: A director at Lantronix will be delivering a speech titled "Out-of-Band Everywhere!" The speech is expected to cover the importance of out-of-band management techniques in various settings. This event, organized by Lantronix, will feature the director discussing the significance of out-of-band management in diverse environments. The speech is scheduled to take place on [date], with an expected audience of [number] attendees. The director's insights will shed light on the practical applications of out-of-band management solutions across different sectors. Attendees can anticipate gaining valuable knowledge on the topic along with real-world examples of its effectiveness.
news.headline: Lantronix is presenting AI-driven LM4 OOB Management Console Servers at Cisco Live San Diego. Director to speak.

news.summary: PyroGenesis announced today that the company has been officially confirmed as an approved supplier for Boeing.
news.headline: PyroGenesis now an approved supplier for Boeing, confirms official status. Exciting news for the company.

news.summary: Seer, Inc. (Nasdaq: SEER), based in Redwood City, California, revealed on May 29, 2025, the launch of its latest Proteograph® Product Suite. This release includes the Proteograph ONE Assay and SP200 Automation Instrument, which mark a significant advancement for the company in the field of high-resolution, mass spectrometry-based proteomics. The unveiling will take place at the American Society for Mass Spectrometry (ASMS) annual conference in Baltimore, MD. This innovation addresses the limitations faced in large-scale proteomic studies due to restricted throughput and high costs. By enhancing the flagship Proteograph Product Suite, Seer is now better equipped to tackle the demands for achieving enhanced throughput, efficiency, and depth necessary for pioneering population-scale, unbiased proteomic investigations.
news.headline: Seer debuts new Proteograph Workflow at ASMS 2025, enhances proteomic studies with scalable, high-res capabilities.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Baker Hughes, a company specializing in energy technology, received a contract from Frontier Infrastructure Holdings for 16 NovaLT™ gas turbines. The agreement, announced in Houston and London, involves powering data center projects in Wyoming and Texas. The turbines are expected to provide a total of 270 megawatts (MW) of power, ensuring efficient and dependable energy supply for the projects. Baker Hughes, traded as BKR on NASDAQ, is set to play a pivotal role in delivering sustainable power solutions to support Frontier's initiatives through this significant partnership.
news.headline: Baker Hughes to supply 16 NovaLT™ gas turbines for Frontier Infrastructure’s U.S. data centers, delivering 270MW power.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Dianthus Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: DNTH), based in New York and Waltham, Mass., revealed its involvement in the Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference. The company, focused on advancing next-gen antibody complement therapeutics for severe autoimmune diseases, disclosed that Marino Garcia, their CEO, will engage in a fireside chat on June 5, 2025 at 9:55 a.m. EDT in New York City. Additionally, Garcia is set to hold individual meetings with investors during the event.
news.headline: Dianthus Therapeutics to present at Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference on June 5, 2025 (Nasdaq: DNTH). CEO Marino Garcia to speak at 9:55 AM EDT in NYC.

news.summary: Kymera Therapeutics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company traded on the NASDAQ as KYMR, based in Watertown, Massachusetts, reported on May 29, 2025, its participation in fireside chats at forthcoming investor events. The company is in the clinical stage and is focused on developing oral small molecule degrader medicines for immunological diseases.
news.headline: Kymera Therapeutics will attend investor conferences in June, focusing on oral small molecule degrader medicines for immunological diseases.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Whitestone REIT (NYSE: WSR) disclosed that CEO Dave Holeman is set to engage in a "Fireside Chat" at Nareit’s 2025 REITweek Investor Conference. The discussion will be moderated by Truist Analyst Anthony Hau.
news.headline: Whitestone REIT's CEO to speak at Nareit’s REITweek 2025 Investor Conference with Truist Analyst Anthony Hau.

news.summary: Dorman Products, Inc.'s Light Duty business division in COLMAR, Pa. revealed the launch of hundreds of fresh automotive repair solutions in May 2025. A significant portion of these are pioneering products in the aftermarket sector. This expansion contributes to Dorman's substantial product range, which now encompasses more than 138,000 SKUs. The newly introduced solutions present about 12 million additional sales prospects for aftermarket parts suppliers, vendors, and maintenance facilities. Furthermore, they provide enhanced accessibility to repair options for car owners.
news.headline: Dorman's Light Duty segment introduces hundreds of new repair solutions, expanding catalog to over 138,000 SKUs, offering 12+ million sales opportunities.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Syndax Pharmaceuticals revealed that CEO Michael A. Metzger and the company's management team will be present at upcoming investor conferences. Syndax, a biopharmaceutical company traded on Nasdaq under the ticker SNDX, focuses on developing groundbreaking cancer treatments.
news.headline: Syndax Pharmaceuticals to participate in investor conferences in June, CEO and team to attend - Michael A. Metzger.

news.summary: Norwalk, Connecticut - On May 29, 2025, FactSet, a leading global financial digital platform and enterprise solutions provider, revealed plans to disclose its financial and operational performance for the third quarter of fiscal 2025, which concludes on May 31, 2025. The data is set to be made public on Monday, June 23, 2025. Additionally, a conference call is scheduled for 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the same day to delve into these outcomes. FactSet is listed on both NYSE (FDS) and NASDAQ (FDS).
news.headline: FactSet to release Q3 fiscal 2025 results on June 23, 2025; earnings call scheduled at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, a biopharmaceutical company called Axsome Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: AXSM) based in New York revealed plans to take part in three investor conferences scheduled for June. The company specializes in treating central nervous system (CNS) disorders and is recognized for pioneering advancements in this field. These conferences will provide an opportunity for the company to engage with investors and share insights into their work.
news.headline: Axsome Therapeutics (NASDAQ: AXSM) to join 3 investor conferences in June; focuses on CNS disorders.

news.summary: On May 29, 2025, Immunovant, Inc. (Nasdaq: IMVT) announced key updates. The company, focused on helping individuals with autoimmune illnesses lead normal lives, shared its corporate and financial performance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending on March 31, 2025.
news.headline: Immunovant (Nasdaq: IMVT) reports Q4 & fiscal year 2025 results, focusing on autoimmune disease treatments.

news.summary: Roivant, a company listed on Nasdaq under the symbol ROIV, has shared its financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and fiscal year concluding on March 31, 2025. The report was released today from locations in Basel, Switzerland, London, and New York. The update includes key figures on the company's performance in the mentioned periods, without specific details given on the results.
news.headline: Roivant (Nasdaq: ROIV) announces Q4 and FY 2025 financial results and business update.

news.headline: tHIS ASEAN 2025 event in Kuala Lumpur from June 9-11 aims to unite healthcare in Southeast Asia.

news.summary: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: EOSE) from Edison, N.J., on May 29, 2025, announced the commencement of a common stock offering worth $75,000,000. This Offering is in accordance with the Securities Act of 1933 and includes a provision for underwriters to potentially purchase an additional $11,250,000 of common stock within 30 days at the offering price, minus underwriting discounts. The completion of the Offering is subject to market conditions and other factors, with no guaranteed assurance of its execution, timing, final size, or terms.
news.headline: Eos Energy Enterprises launches $75 million common stock offering with possible additional $11.25 million.

news.summary: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. has disclosed plans to offer $175 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers. The offering is subject to market conditions and regulatory requirements. Additionally, the company intends to provide initial purchasers of the notes with an option to buy up to an extra $26.25 million aggregate principal amount of notes within 13 days from the issuance date.
news.headline: Eos Energy plans $175M convertible notes offering due 2030, with an option for an additional $26.25M.

news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of May 29, 2025, a recent report has indicated a significant growth in the 3D Printing Metals Market. The report focuses on various metal types including Titanium, Aluminum, Steel, Nickel & Cobalt, among others. These metals are utilized in forms such as filaments and powder. Technologies such as Powder Bed Fusion, Directed Energy Deposition, Binder Jetting, and Metal Extrusion are employed in this market. This growing sector reflects the increasing demand for advanced manufacturing techniques.
news.headline: 3D Printing Metals Market projected to reach $3.62 billion by 2030, as per MarketsandMarkets™ report.

news.summary: Movado Group, Inc. (NYSE: MOV) released their first quarter fiscal 2026 results, covering the period up to April 30, 2025. The highlights show a slight decline in net sales, with $131.8 million in the current quarter compared to $134.4 million last year. The gross margin decreased to 54.1% from 54.3% in the previous year. Operating income saw a significant decrease to $0.3 million, which includes $0.6 million in expenses associated with a cost-saving initiative, in contrast to $2.1 million in the prior year's period.
news.headline: Movado Group, Inc. reports Q1 Fiscal 2026 results: $131.8M net sales, 54.1% gross margin, $0.3M operating income.

news.summary: In St. Louis, Caleres (NYSE: CAL), a prominent collection of consumer-focused footwear labels, has announced its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025. Their sales reached $614.2 million, marking a 6.8% decrease compared to the previous year, falling short of expectations. Notably, Famous Footwear saw a sales decline of 6.3%, with a 4.6% drop in comparable sales from the first quarter of 2024. The Brand Portfolio also experienced a 6.9% decrease in sales compared to the same period last year. Earnings for this quarter were reported at $0.21 per diluted share, with adjusted earnings slightly higher at $0.22.
news.headline: Caleres reports Q1 2025 results: Sales $614.2M, down 6.8% YoY; Famous Footwear sales down 6.3%, brand portfolio sales decreased 6.9%. Earnings per diluted share $0.21.

news.summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. (NYSE: BBW) revealed its fiscal quarter results for the period ending May 3, 2025, in St. Louis. The company reported total revenues of $128.4 million, marking an 11.9% increase, while pre-tax income reached $19.6 million, up by 30.6% from the previous year. Diluted earnings per share also saw a significant surge, hitting $1.17, an increase of 42.7%. Additionally, in the same period, the company returned $7.1 million to its shareholders through share repurchase programs.
news.headline: Build-A-Bear Workshop's Q1 FY2025 results: Record $128.4M revenue (11.9% increase), $19.6M pre-tax income (30.6% increase), $1.17 EPS (42.7% increase).
